with open(path2, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text)

import re
text = text

# Find citations within curly braces
brace_spans = [(m.start(), m.end(), m.group()) for m in re.finditer(r"\{[^}]*\}", text, flags=re.DOTALL)]
brace_spans

m = re.search(r"وَقَالَ لَهُمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ[^\n]*", text)
m.start(), m.end(), m.group()[:50]

queries: وَإِذِ اِستَسَخَشَت رِجَالٌ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْقُرَىٰ فَخَرَجُوا بِالْفَجْرِ خِفْيَةً قلوبهم مشتركة بخطيئة قالوا لا تأتنا بالمسامع أو نزلنا سوءا قال لو أنا إلا نذير لكم بل رسول قد بشرت له رحمة من ربه قضاء مختما وإن تصدقوه يضاعف لكم من قبل أن يأتي عليكم وإن تكفروا فإن عليكم عذاب يوم عظيم
وَقَالَ لَهُمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ يَا قَوْمِ لَوْ آمَنْتُمْ بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ لَأُتِمَّتْ لَكُمْ أُجُورُكُمْ وَلَكِنْ كَذَّبْتُمْ فَسَوْفَ يَأْتِيَنَّكُمْ عَذَابِ الْحَصِيرِ
وَإِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ رُسُلُهُمْ فَكَلِمَةُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا تَصْلَيَانِ
وَإِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ رُسُلُهُمْ أَفَلَا تَتُوبُونَ لِتُجْزَوْا خَلِيلًا

وَإِذْ قَالَ لَهُمْ رُسُلُهُمْ يَا قَوْمِ إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ يَأْتِيَكُمْ لِيُجْزِزَ لَكُمْ عُقُدَاتِكُمْ وَلِيُخْرِجَ الَّذِينَ يُزَكِّيكُمْ وَيُطَهِّرُكُمْ وَيُعَلِّمُكُمْ مِنْ آيَاتِ اللَّهِ وَيُعَلِّمُكُمْ الْحِكْمَةَ وَيُعَلِّمُكُمْ مِنْ أَمْرِ اللَّهِ وَيُخْرِجُ عَنْكُمْ رِجَالًا وَجُنَّةً وَيُدَخِلُ عَلَيْكُمْ رِجَالًا وَاللَّهُ بَصِيرٌ بِالْعِبَادِ
قُتِلَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ عُزَّى جلا جلاله من قوم كثر عليهم النصب صحيح البخاري
هل توجد كلمة تصليان في القرآن
أفلا تتوبون لتجزوا خليلا في القرآن
الحاقة 19 فأما من أوتي كتابه بيمينه
سورة الحاقة آية 19 فأما من أوتي كتابه بيمينه
الحاقة 25 وأما من أوتي كتابه بشماله

حديث النصب نصْب صحيح البخاري أبو هريرة

output.code:
import json, re

path2 = "/mnt/data/file-QDJQs1LyHQT6F5MS9azX4c-Q042.txt"
with open(path2, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()

# Collect spans:
spans = []

# 1) Curly braces blocks
for m in re.finditer(r"\{[^}]*\}", text, flags=re.DOTALL):
    spans.append((m.start(), m.end(), m.group(), "Aya"))

# 2) The unbraced Quran line
m = re.search(r"وَقَالَ لَهُمْ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ[^\n]*", text)
if m:
    spans.append((m.start(), m.end(), m.group(), "Aya"))

# 3) The hadith quote starting with « until EOF (since no closing »)
start = text.find('«')
if start != -1:
    end = text.find('»', start+1)
    if end == -1:
        end = len(text)
    else:
        end = end + 1
    spans.append((start, end, text[start:end], "Hadeeth"))

# sort spans by start
spans.sort(key=lambda x: x[0])

spans

citations = []
for start, end, s, typ in spans:
    item = {
        "span_start": start,
        "span_end": end,
        "span_text": s,
        "citation_type": "Aya" if typ == "Aya" else "Hadeeth",
        "is_valid": False,
        "aya_reference": {} if typ == "Aya" else {},
        "hadeeth_reference": {} if typ == "Hadeeth" else {},
        "corrected_text": "خطأ",
        "completely_invalid": True
    }
    citations.append(item)

output = {"citations": citations}
print(json.dumps(output, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
